Up (2009)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $293,004,164Details
International Box Office $438,459,213
Worldwide Box Office $731,463,377
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $224,426,773
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $83,404,199
Total $307,830,972
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Carl Fredricksen, a 78-year-old balloon salesman, is about to fulfill a lifelong dream. Tying thousands of balloons to his house, he flies away to the South American wilderness. But curmudgeonly Carl’s worst nightmare comes true when he discovers a little boy named Russell is a stowaway aboard the balloon-powered house.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$68,108,790 (23.2% of total gross)
Legs:4.30 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:40.1% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$175,000,000 (worldwide box office is 4.2 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,766 opening theaters/3,886 max. theaters, 7.8 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $467,243,975

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: May 29th, 2009 (Wide) by Walt Disney
International Releases: May 28th, 2009 (Wide) (Australia)
July 29th, 2009 (Wide) (South Korea)
September 10th, 2009 (Wide) (New Zealand)
March 21st, 2015 (Wide), released as Up (RE: 2015) (Australia)
January 25th, 2023 (Limited) (South Korea)
Video Release: November 10th, 2009 by Walt Disney Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG for some peril and action
Running Time: 89 minutes
Award nomination: 2010 Oscars Best Picture Nominee
Cast, crew, or production detail: Animal Lead, Prologue
Film format: 3-D
Plot point: Death of a Spouse or Fiancée / Fiancé, Infertility, Romance, Talking Animals, Widow/Widower
Subgenre: Family Adventure
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Adventure
Production Method:Digital Animation
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Walt Disney Pictures, Disney-Pixar
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Carl Fredricksen, a 78-year-old balloon salesman, is about to fulfill a lifelong dream. Tying thousands of balloons to his house, he flies away to the South American wilderness. But curmudgeonly Carl’s worst nightmare comes true when he discovers a little boy named Russell is a stowaway aboard the balloon-powered house.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$68,108,790 (23.2% of total gross)
Legs:4.30 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:40.1% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$175,000,000 (worldwide box office is 4.2 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,766 opening theaters/3,886 max. theaters, 7.8 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $467,243,975
